Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that 1 Orders events by timestamp within each case_id to get activity sequences 2 Groups cases by their complete activity sequence to identify variants 3 Counts cases per variant and finds the top K variants by frequency 4 Returns all events from the original event log that belong to cases which are part of these top K variants

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 1 Construct the activity sequence per case_id We can use STRING AGG activity ORDER BY timestamp or similar Since the prompt says ordered sequence STRING AGG is fine

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Lets use STRING AGG activity comma ORDER BY timestamp as a string representation This is simple and standard SQL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 2 Group cases by variant and count We need a CTE or subquery that computes the variant per case then groups by variant to count cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 3 Determine top K variants In DuckDB we can use ORDER BY case count DESC LIMIT K

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 4 Return all events from original event log for cases in top K variants We need to return the original columns for events belonging to cases whose variant is in the top K

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: This seems straightforward and correct But wait what about ties If we want to be precise about ties we could use RANK or DENSE RANK

Name: Validation - IND
Text: The prompt says top K variants by their frequency Usually LIMIT K is sufficient

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets keep it simple but robust I will present the query using STRING AGG and a LIMIT K wrapped in a parameter CTE

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me review step by step 1 Construct sequence per case case variants CTE does this with STRING AGG ORDER BY timestamp 2 Group by variant top k variants CTE groups by the variant string and counts 3 Top K ORDER BY case count DESC LIMIT K 4 Return events Join event log with case variants and filter where variant is in top k variants Everything looks correct
